description_long: This is a tall, industrial-style chain-link fence panel topped with a coil of barbed or razor wire, designed for high-security applications. The main body consists of a diamond-patterned metal mesh held within a sturdy rectangular metal frame, supported by two vertical posts on either side. The upper edge of the fence is lined with a continuous spiral of sharp wire, which acts as a deterrent to climbing. The posts are capped with simple, flat ends, and the overall construction is utilitarian, prioritizing function over decoration. The mesh is tightly woven, providing little space for handholds or footholds. This type of fence is commonly found around restricted urban areas, such as utility sites, parking lots, or secure perimeters.
description: A tall, chain-link security fence with a barbed wire coil on top and a sturdy metal frame. Utilitarian and designed for high-security urban environments.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the full rectangular mesh panel and the barbed wire coil running along the top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the posts and the spiral of barbed wire above the mesh.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually similar to the front, displaying the mesh and barbed wire coil.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the post and barbed wire coil profile.
